channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: USPS agrees to work with DOGE on reform, planning to cut 10,000 workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Postmaster General Louis DeJoy plans to cut 10,000 workers and billions of dollars from the U.S. Postal Service budget and he’ll do that working with Elon Musk ’s Department of Government Efficiency, according to a letter sent to members of Congress on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: VIDEO: American Airlines plane evacuated in Denver after fire aboard
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, ABC 4

item.title: Schumer to advance GOP funding bill, unwilling to risk government shutdown as deadline nears
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Democratic leader Chuck Schumer relented Thursday rather than risk a government shutdown, announcing he's ready to start the process of considering a Republican-led government funding bill that has fiercely divided Democrats under pressure to impose limits on the Trump admin
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mexicans searching for missing relatives uncover possible mass killing site in Jalisco state
item.description: TEUCHITLAN, Mexico (AP) — When a group of citizens searching for missing relatives in the western state of Jalisco arrived at a remote ranch outside Mexico's second-largest city last week on an anonymous tip, all they had to do was push open the unlocked gate.
item.dc:creator: DIANA DE LA MORA and FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Columbia University says it expelled some students who seized building last year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University has expelled or suspended some students who took over a campus building during pro-Palestinian protests last spring and temporarily revoked the diplomas of others who have since graduated, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Member of '90s girl group En Vogue living in car for 3 years
item.description: A member of the once popular R&B girl group En Vogue just revealed she has been living in her car for the past three years.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls 61K sparkling water bottles
item.description: Trader Joe's announced a recall of two lots of the popular Gerolsteiner Sparkling Natural Mineral Water, a non-Trader Joe's branded item, which the grocery giant sells.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Parents arrested after refusing child a ride to school; found walking on highway
item.description: The Midland Police Department arrested two people earlier this month after a young girl was found walking alone on a busy street in the early morning hours.
item.dc:creator: Gabriella Meza, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Khalil protestors fill up Trump Tower
item.description: Protesters opposed to the detention of Palestinian activist Mahmoud Khalil filled up Trump Tower in New York on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Connecticut woman arrested after stepson allegedly held captive for over 20 years
item.description: A Connecticut woman accused of kidnapping a man and holding him captive for over 20 years was arrested Wednesday, police said.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Lepore, ABC 4

item.title: Third MLB hat pulled over accidental vulgarity as others sell for hundreds online
item.description: Yet another newly released MLB hat has been pulled from online stores after fans noted the potentially crass design the cap sported.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal student loan site down Wednesday, a day after layoffs gutted Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An hours-long outage Wednesday on StudentAid.gov, the federal website for student loans and financial aid, underscored the risks in rapidly gutting the Department of Education, as President Donald Trump aims to dismantle the agency.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge rules Trump must reinstate many fired federal employees
item.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday that the Trump administration must reinstate probationary government employees fired unlawfully at several agencies, lambasting the Justice Department at a hearing for a “sham” gambit that enabled a key official to avoid testifying in the case. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Lawsuit accuses man of killing, eating patients at SC mental health facility
item.description: The case against a South Carolina mental health facility where a man is accused of murdering and cannibalizing patients could be heading to trial.
item.dc:creator: Chase Laudenslager, ABC 4

item.title: Pete Buttigieg won't seek US Senate seat in Michigan, leaving door wide open for 2028 run
item.description: SAUGATUCK, Mich. (AP) — Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg announced that he will not seek an open U.S. Senate seat in Michigan, a move that leaves the door wide open for him to seek a much bigger role as his party’s next presidential nominee.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pete Buttigieg declines Senate run in Michigan
item.description: Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg announced he will not run for Senate in Michigan, keeping the door open for a potential presidential run in 2028.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WGN 9

item.title: Justice Department launches criminal investigation into 2 NYC migrant shelters
item.description: The Justice Department is investigating funding and operations at two Manhattan hotels being used as migrant shelters, according to several reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Explosion at Texas Tech causes evacuations, immediate spring break
item.description: An explosion at a substation on the Texas Tech campus has caused power outages at the Texas Tech and the Health Sciences Center Wednesday evening, according to a TechAlert.
item.dc:creator: Georgina Johnston, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Columbia anti-Israel protest ringleader Mahmoud Khalil to remain detained in Louisiana
item.description: Mahmoud Khalil, one of the ringleaders of anti-Israel protests at Columbia University last year, will remain in ICE custody for now following a hearing in a Manhattan federal court on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delphi murders: Full 'Bridge Guy' video releases on website supporting Richard Allen
item.description: (WXIN/WTTV) — A full, 43-second video taken by Libby German just before she and her friend Abby Williams were murdered has surfaced online. The video was a crucial piece of evidence in the Delphi murders trial, which ended in the conviction of suspect Richard Allen.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, WFLA, NBC

item.title: From soup cans to airplanes, steel and aluminum are a fundamental part of American life
item.description: Steel and aluminum are ubiquitous in Americans' lives. A stainless steel refrigerator holds aluminum soda cans. A stainless steel drum tumbles inside an aluminum washing machine. They're the metals used in cars and airplanes, phones and frying pans, skyscrapers and zippers.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Marine mammals in California getting sick from algae: Care center
item.description: "The number of current MMCC patients likely impacted by the toxic algal bloom has more than tripled," Marine Mammal Care said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas Strip hotel to be redesigned by reality TV star
item.description: Lisa Vanderpump announced Wednesday she plans to completely renovate a Strip hotel, transforming it into the "Vanderpump Hotel.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's CEO wants to speed up the process it takes to introduce new burgers
item.description: McDonald’s CEO Chris Kempczinski said he intends to introduce new burgers and improve the chain’s signature burgers at a faster rate.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: ICE runs out of space, money to detain migrants, report says
item.description: A high-ranking official with Immigration and Customs Enforcement says the agency has run out of money to detain and house more migrants for the time being, according to Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Affordable housing threatened as Trump halts $1 billion slated for extending life of aging buildings
item.description: The Trump administration is halting a $1 billion program that helps preserve affordable housing, threatening projects that keep tens of thousands of units livable for low-income Americans, according to a document obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Education Department layoffs gut its civil rights office, leaving discrimination cases in limbo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department’s civil rights branch is losing nearly half its staff in the Trump administration’s layoffs, effectively gutting an office that already faced a backlog of thousands of complaints from students and families across the nation.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal judge appears skeptical probationary firings were for performance
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge repeatedly sounded skeptical on Wednesday that the Trump administration’s mass firings of probationary federal workers were made by the government because the employees couldn’t do their jobs, saying the terminations appeared to be part of a larger goal. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Education secretary: Mass layoffs first step toward total shutdown
item.description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon affirmed late Tuesday that mass layoffs are the first step toward shuttering the Education Department — a long-time goal of President Donald Trump's since his time on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: EU retaliates as Trump steel, aluminum tariffs take effect
item.description: After announcing the response, the EU released a 99-page list of possible items that could be subject to tariffs, including fruit and vegetables, meat, alcoholic beverages, nicotine vapes and chewing gum.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Most AAPI adults don't support cutting agencies and want a focus on costs: AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders do not agree with the strategy of putting entire federal agencies on the chopping block, nor are they broadly on board with mass layoffs of federal workers, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US bans flights to Haiti's capital until Sept. 8 as UN expert says gang violence is more dire
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Tuesday extended its ban on flights to Haiti’s capital until Sept. 8 because of escalating gang violence, which the U.N.’s human rights expert on the Caribbean nation said is more dire than ever.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: VIDEO: Masked 'Box Demon' spooks homeowner
item.description: Police are searching for a suspicious person who wore a mask and approached a homeowner's door while referring to themselves as the "Box Demon" in York County, Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NOAA set to fire 1,029 more employees
item.description: Staffing levels at the nation's already hobbled weather, climate and oceans agency could take a further nosedive in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former US Rep. Katie Porter steps into crowded California governor's race
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former Democratic U.S. House member Katie Porter announced Tuesday that she is entering the 2026 contest for California governor, joining a crowded field of candidates that could be upended if former Vice President Kamala Harris joins the race.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bags fly free no more: Southwest Airlines to end free checked luggage policy
item.description: Southwest Airlines will no longer allow bags to fly free. The airline announced Tuesday it will begin charging some customers who check baggage in May.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, Fox 5
